meta.title: Consolidated Reconciliation Ledger (Scind ↔ Xcind sync)
meta.description: Merges every P3 (learnings), P4 (xcind-ahead) and P5 (scind-ahead) action item into one ordered ledger. Join key = source-finding ID (L-/XA-/SA-/RG-). Scind-affecting rows are fully specified with target file + edit sketch and marked PLANNED, then flipped to APPLIED once landed in the Scind PR. DIVERGENCE / DELIBERATELY-DEFERRED rows are referenced by origin ID and owned by P7 (engineering/sync/divergence/) — this ledger authors no divergence entries.

action_types.CANON-CHANGE: Edit existing Scind canon (P3 learning proved the design wrong/incomplete).
action_types.PROMOTE: Add a new capability to Scind canon (P4 Xcind-ahead).
action_types.CANON-OVERREACH: Trim/fix over-specified Scind canon (P5 reverse learning).
action_types.CANON-CONFIRM: Annotate a Scind ADR/spec as validated by Xcind (low priority).
action_types.DIVERGENCE: Record in the P7 divergence registry (P3/P4 DIVERGENCE, P5 DELIBERATELY-DEFERRED).
action_types.XCIND-BACKLOG: Xcind issue/todo (P5 NOT-IMPLEMENTED, IMPLEMENTED-UNTESTED, shared-future work).
action_types.PROCESS: P6 procedure / Xcind doc maintenance; does not touch Scind design.
action_types.ESCALATE: Human product call; §2 could not be cleanly applied.

status_values.PLANNED: Scind-affecting, fully specified; awaiting/landing in the Scind PR.
status_values.APPLIED: Landed in the Scind reconciliation branch/PR (see scind_pr).
status_values.DEFERRED-TO-P7: Divergence — owned by the P7 registry (engineering/sync/divergence/).

planned_scind_changes.note: 8 CANON-CHANGE↔PROMOTE pairs target the same Scind edit (L-0001/XA-0002, L-0003/XA-0003, L-0004/XA-0017, L-0005/XA-0001, L-0008/XA-0005, L-0010/XA-0004, L-0011/XA-0007, L-0014/XA-0018); apply each once. Distinct Scind edit-sets ≈ substantive_total − 8.

rows.title: ≥2-label proxy-domain constraint for wildcard TLS
rows.edit_sketch: Add constraint: any proxy domain used for wildcard TLS MUST contain ≥1 dot (≥2 labels) under non-disabled TLS; strict RFC-6125 stacks reject *.singlelabel even with a valid SAN; implementations SHOULD warn (not fail) on a single-label domain. Optional HSTS-TLD caveat.
rows.notes: Duplicate of PROMOTE XA-0002 — apply once. Headline canon-change.

rows.title: Project-name isolation must handle multiple working copies (worktrees)
rows.edit_sketch: State {workspace}-{application} assumes one working copy; introduce a per-instance disambiguation token that EXTENDS the project name (empty token = current behavior, backward compatible); fold into project-name + internal-network patterns; resolution order explicit > opt-out > auto-detect-from-linked-worktree.
rows.notes: Xcind source = ADR-0019. Example-B calibration case.

rows.title: Assigned exports need three vars (_HOST + _PORT + _HOST_PORT)
rows.edit_sketch: Split assigned-export vars into _HOST (in-network alias) + _PORT (container port) + _HOST_PORT (allocated host port). Current _HOST/_PORT pair is self-contradictory when host port != container port.
rows.notes: Duplicate of PROMOTE XA-0003 — apply once. Defect fix, not mere promotion.

rows.title: Service discovery must offer a host-view rendering
rows.edit_sketch: Add Host-View Environment section: opt-in host-view env file whose assigned exports use 127.0.0.1 + host port while proxied/apex keep the routable hostname, from the SAME computation as container injection; document dotenv/compose precedence; explicit own/block write mode.
rows.notes: Duplicate of PROMOTE XA-0017. Depends on L-0003. If Scind rules host-side access out of scope → downgrade to divergence.

rows.title: Assigned-port artifacts are live-state-derived, not a pure config function
rows.target: scind: docs/specs/port-types.md (+ workspace-lifecycle.md Staleness Detection, state-management.md)
rows.edit_sketch: State assigned port values resolve against live global port state (not a pure function of app config); add global assigned-ports state as a regeneration trigger; define an always-regenerate class for live-state-derived override content.
rows.notes: Duplicate of PROMOTE XA-0001. Cache-soundness cluster with L-0006/L-0007.

rows.title: Generated artifacts embedding assigned values must be written AFTER allocation
rows.edit_sketch: Add explicit assigned-port allocation/validation step; require it to precede override (step 8) + manifest (step 10) writes; add manifest freshness contract (written after allocation, reflects post-allocation values).

rows.title: Staleness must catch partial output + generator-version changes
rows.edit_sketch: Require atomic generation (temp + rename) with a completeness marker; treat partial/incomplete output as stale; add generator/schema version to staleness inputs; a failed resolve step must fail generation, not persist a truncated artifact.

rows.title: Specify HTTP→HTTPS redirect middleware + every-service-block constraint
rows.edit_sketch: Specify redirect via a redirectscheme middleware on the HTTP router; note the middleware DEFINITION must be repeated on every rendered service block because Traefik's Docker provider reads labels only from running containers (idempotent repetition).
rows.notes: Overlaps PROMOTE XA-0005 (per-export tls). Apply together.

rows.title: Traefik static config emits websecure/file-provider/cert mounts only when TLS enabled
rows.edit_sketch: Annotate the websecure entrypoint, file provider, :443 port and ./certs//./dynamic mounts as emitted only when tls.mode != disabled (mirroring the already-conditional dashboard).

rows.title: Proxy HTTP/HTTPS/dashboard ports should be configurable
rows.target: scind: docs/specs/configuration-schemas.md proxy schema + traefik-compose appendix
rows.edit_sketch: Add proxy.http_port / proxy.https_port (reuse proxy.dashboard.port); reference them in the appendix ports: block instead of literal 80/443/8080.

rows.title: Add preferred-scheme .url label
rows.edit_sketch: Add scind.export.{name}.proxy.url / scind.apex.proxy.url (HTTPS when an HTTPS router exists else HTTP), additive to the per-protocol URL labels.

rows.title: Single --json contract backs labels+introspection; reads side-effect-free
rows.target: scind: docs/reference/cli.md (or a new specs contract doc)
rows.edit_sketch: (1) Define a stable --json introspection contract (assignedExports/proxiedExports keyed by export name) shared by Traefik label generation and read/show commands; (2) state read-only commands must resolve from persisted/config state and never trigger generation, cert provisioning, or proxy startup.

rows.title: Reporting/introspection should prefer the apex URL
rows.edit_sketch: scind urls / app show report the apex URL for the primary proxied export (per-export URL kept in detail view); add apex/apex_host as additive JSON fields from the same source as scind.apex.* labels.
rows.notes: Apex cluster (with L-0018, L-0028). Absorbs P4 ESCALATE XA-0025 handed back to P3.

rows.title: First-class app-level env-file injection into all services
rows.edit_sketch: Add app-level env-file injection (compose_env_files for --env-file interpolation vs app_env_files generating env_file: on every service); document the two-scope Compose distinction explicitly.

rows.title: Re-running a config-writing command must preserve unrelated user fields
rows.edit_sketch: State that re-running workspace init (or any command writing workspace.yaml/application.yaml) performs targeted field updates and preserves unrelated user-authored fields, extending the overrides/ preservation guarantee to the primary config files.

rows.title: Validate resolved/inferred port values at generation time
rows.edit_sketch: Add a port-value validation step (resolved/inferred ports must be integers 1–65535, protocol suffixes handled explicitly); invalid ports fail at generation with an export-named error, not downstream in Traefik.

rows.title: External-network creation failures must be surfaced, not swallowed
rows.edit_sketch: Startup steps 1–2: network/proxy creation failures must emit a diagnostic naming the resource + underlying error (the overlay declares it external, so compose otherwise fails opaquely); define whether the failure is fatal to up.

rows.title: Apex implicit-primary scoped to PROXIED exports
rows.edit_sketch: Refine implicit-primary from 'exactly one exported service' to 'exactly one PROXIED exported service ⇒ implicitly primary (apex-eligible)'; assigned exports don't count against eligibility. Keep explicit primary:true required only when 2+ proxied exports compete.
rows.notes: Apex cluster. Adversarial re-check SPLIT this (order-independent half).

rows.title: Apex selection HYBRID: explicit primary:true, else positional fallback
rows.edit_sketch: Amend to a HYBRID rule: explicit primary:true always wins; when none marked, fall back to POSITIONAL (first-declared proxied export) and still emit an apex, instead of 'none marked → no apex'. Requires giving exported_services a defined declaration order (sequence, or documented first-declared rule).
rows.notes: Apex cluster. HUMAN PRODUCT-CALL overrode a provisional divergence. Resolves SA-0006 overreach candidacy.

rows.title: Generation-cache live-state correctness
rows.edit_sketch: Staleness/regeneration must distinguish config-derived override content (cacheable) from live-state-derived content (assigned host ports, discovery env vars) and refresh the latter even when the config-based staleness check reports up-to-date.

rows.title: Proxy domain must be multi-label
rows.edit_sketch: Same as L-0001: mandate ≥1 dot in the proxy domain + non-fatal startup warning under non-disabled TLS; cite RFC-6125 strict-vs-lenient matching (ADR-0016 evidence).

rows.title: _HOST_PORT assigned-export discovery var
rows.edit_sketch: Add SCIND_{APP}_{SERVICE}_HOST_PORT (allocated host-published port) alongside in-network _HOST/_PORT.

rows.title: Configurable proxy host ports
rows.edit_sketch: Add SCIND_PROXY_HTTP_PORT/HTTPS_PORT/DASHBOARD_PORT; specify discovery _PORT/_URL include the port when non-default. Defaults unchanged (80/443/8080).

rows.title: Per-export TLS metadata + redirect middleware pattern
rows.edit_sketch: Add a per-export tls attribute (auto/require/disable); document the redirect-middleware pattern (shared middleware repeated on every service block; redirect-only routers point at noop@internal).

rows.title: traefik.docker.network label + pinned router→service binding
rows.edit_sketch: Add traefik.docker.network={proxy-network} to required routing labels and document explicit router→loadbalancer-service binding (multi-network containers otherwise mis-route intermittently — a correctness bug given ADR-0002).

rows.title: Preferred scheme-agnostic .url label
rows.edit_sketch: Add a canonical scind.export.{name}.url / scind.apex.url (https when an HTTPS router serves the export else http), additive to per-protocol labels.

rows.title: TLS cert cascade + domain-change regeneration marker
rows.edit_sketch: Specify the auto-mode resolution order (user cert > cached > mkcert > openssl self-signed) and require regenerating the wildcard when the configured domain changes (recorded-domain marker), else a stale-CN cert is served.

rows.title: Path-existence GC of assigned ports
rows.edit_sketch: Add a GC rule: drop an assigned-port entry whose recorded application path no longer exists, run on the port-inventory maintenance commands.

rows.title: Context-aware shell prompt segment
rows.edit_sketch: Add a scind prompt command + a generate path emitting a Starship snippet; specify the fast context-only detection path and --print field / --detect / apex-url (OSC 8) semantics.
rows.notes: Scind shell-integration currently has no prompt concept at all.

rows.title: Per-app export introspection (urls/ports/exports, scalar output)
rows.edit_sketch: Add scind app urls/ports/exports with a [SERVICE] filter and -q/scalar output for scripting; align JSON shape with assignedExports/proxiedExports maps.

rows.title: Unified per-export descriptor
rows.edit_sketch: Define a unified export descriptor merging assigned + proxied by export name (type/service/port/url/tls/apex) in one view; expose via app show / exports.

rows.title: Resolved/flattened compose-config export
rows.edit_sketch: Add a resolved/flattened compose-config output (stdout + arbitrary file), the substrate for the Dev Container workflow; document the devcontainer consumer. CLI shape (subcommand vs flag) is Scind's to decide.

rows.title: POSIX docker/compose wrapper generation
rows.edit_sketch: Add a wrapper-script generator emitting real executable docker/docker-compose wrappers for tools (JetBrains, CI) that exec a literal binary and cannot call the scind-compose shell function.

rows.title: Build provenance in --version
rows.edit_sketch: Specify a SemVer build-metadata suffix (+source.rev.dirty.date) populated at build time. Mechanism (env-var injection) is Bash-flavored/excluded (§5); the capability (provenance disclosure) promotes.

rows.title: Fine-grained registry ops: register/forget PATH
rows.edit_sketch: Add scind workspace register PATH (cloned-but-never-run workspace, no Docker labels) and scind workspace forget PATH (drop one moved/deleted entry).

rows.title: Host/container env symmetry
rows.target: scind: docs/specs/environment-variables.md + new ADR (analogous to Scind ADR-0018)
rows.edit_sketch: Add a host-view env export: opt-in host-flavored dotenv emitting SCIND_{APP}_{EXPORT}_* with loopback IP + published host port for assigned exports and the routable hostname for proxied/apex; own/block write modes.
rows.notes: Same edit as CANON-CHANGE L-0004. Self-flagged PROMOTE in Xcind ADR-0020.

rows.title: App-level env-file interpolation-vs-injection split
rows.edit_sketch: Add compose_env_files (--env-file interpolation) and app_env_files (env_file: injection into all services) to application.yaml.

rows.title: Two-track documentation (Diátaxis + LDS)
rows.target: scind: rename docs/ → engineering/ (+ optional placeholder docs/README.md)
rows.edit_sketch: Rename Scind's design-canon tree docs/ → engineering/ (matching Xcind's LDS track); optionally add a placeholder docs/README.md stating intent for a future user-facing Diátaxis track. No Diátaxis content exists yet in either project.
rows.notes: HUMAN PRODUCT-CALL. Structural/repo-wide change — deferred in this PR (see notes); recommend a separate Scind PR to avoid churning every doc path mid-review. STATUS: still PLANNED — deferred to a separate Scind PR (repo-wide rename churns every doc path mid-review).

rows.title: XDG Base Directory split (config vs state)
rows.edit_sketch: Default SCIND_STATE_DIR to $XDG_STATE_HOME/scind (fallback ~/.local/state/scind); keep SCIND_CONFIG_DIR under $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/scind; align with the XDG spec (config vs state vs data). Encodes ADR-0005 at the filesystem level.

rows.title: Fail-fast port-conflict at startup mis-fires on idempotent re-up
rows.edit_sketch: Startup port-conflict check must exclude ports bound by the workspace's OWN running containers before declaring a conflict, else idempotent workspace up reports false conflicts (the flapping Xcind hit). Verify Scind's port_inventory + bind-probe attributes a bound port to its owner.
rows.notes: Reverse learning (Xcind→Scind). Confidence medium — hinges on Scind's port_inventory ownership attribution.

rows.title: SCIND_* discovery injection schema build-validated
rows.edit_sketch: Annotate that the SCIND_{APP}_{EXPORT}_{SUFFIX} injection schema (env-safing, HTTPS-default, apex vars) is build-validated by the Xcind PoC. No behavioral change.
rows.notes: Low priority annotation. _HOST/_HOST_PORT defect is L-0003, separate.

rows.title: Derived-not-tracked application model validated
rows.edit_sketch: Optionally note the derived-not-tracked application model (apps resolved from workspace directory contents, no registry) is PoC-validated.

rows.title: Workspace state machine is descriptive; runtime inference sufficient
rows.edit_sketch: Keep the state table as descriptive prose; optionally add a note that the PoC confirmed no stored state artifact is needed to drive lifecycle transitions.

rows.title: Registry + sticky assigned-port state are load-bearing (confirms ADR-0005)
rows.edit_sketch: No design change — record that Xcind's attempt to out-stateless Scind failed: a workspace registry + sticky assigned-port state are load-bearing, confirming ADR-0005. (Xcind's stale 'stateless' wording is an Xcind-maintenance item.)

rows.title: Pervasive eng-doc↔code drift → periodic source-review sweep

rows.title: Make the flavors deferral explicit in Xcind ADR-0005

rows.title: ADR numbering & cross-referencing policy
rows.edit_sketch: Recommend Option A (topic-keyed cross-reference table, no renumbering) hosted in Xcind engineering/sync/. Divergence-encoding ADRs marked and pointed at the P7 registry. Written as Xcind ADR-0021 by this plan.
